message:
test(e2e): generalize hero-overlap specs to cover all 8 articles and measure against hero content bottom

Rewrite `_diag-scroll-indicator.spec.ts` and `_diag-scroll-indicator-prod.spec.ts`
so the overlap guardrail covers every article that uses the
hero "scroll indicator" pattern — not just the five I happened to
identify initially by grepping for the literal "Scroll to Explore".

Articles covered (8 total, up from 5):
  Scroll to Explore     : wills-and-estate-planning, slack-mattermost-migration,
                          overprompting, raptorq, barra-factor-model
  Scroll to Discover    : bakery_algorithm
  Scroll to Optimize    : cmaes_explainer
  Initialization        : hoeffdings_d_explainer

Two substantive changes:

1. Measure against hero's own content container, not against stat chips.

   Some articles (bakery, cmaes, barra) don't have stat chips inside
   the hero at all — the previous chip-based selector either silently
   skipped the assertion (false green) or matched a chip from deeper
   in the article body (false red on barra). The new approach
   iterates the hero element's direct children, skips any that are
   position: absolute / fixed or that contain the indicator itself,
   and takes the max `.bottom` of the remainder as `contentBottom`.
   That is a structural property of the hero layout independent of
   which bit of text the article happens to render.

2. Sweep three narrow viewports per article (320×568, 360×640, 375×667).

   The original spec only exercised 375×800, which already had enough
   vertical room for the cmaes/bakery/hoeffding heroes — so the
   overlap only manifested on tighter phones. Running the matrix at
   three realistic viewport widths catches the overlap at its worst
   case (e.g. bakery_algorithm at 320×568 overlaps by −70px without
   the fix) and confirms a consistent post-fix gap of 48px (== Tailwind
   `mt-12`, 3rem).

Also add `test.describe.configure({ mode: "serial" })` to both specs.
Running 24 navigations to the local Next dev server in parallel causes
cold-compile misses where `document.querySelector('[data-section="hero"]')`
returns null; serial execution removes that flake entirely (local:
24/24 pass in 7m total; individual tests take ~15s each).

The prod spec picks 360×640 as the single worst-case viewport and runs
one test per article — 8 tests, all strict-asserting `indicator.top >=
contentBottom - 1`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
